message:
fix: apply inverse rotation in decode() and rotate query in dot()

decode() returned the polar reconstruction in rotated space without
applying R^T, and dot() computed the polar dot product with an
unrotated query against rotated data. Both produced near-random
results (cosine similarity ~0.003 between original and decoded).

Changes:
- decode(): combine polar + QJL residual in rotated space, then
  apply R^T to the sum to return to original space
- dot(): rotate query with R before polar and QJL dot products
- Add qjl.decodeIntoRotated() for decode path (no R^T in QJL)
- qjl.estimateDotWithWorkspace() now accepts pre-rotated query

Results: cosine similarity 0.003 → 0.965, dot error 0.063 → 0.012.

New tests:
- "decode cosine similarity": verifies avg cosine sim > 0.5 across dims
- "dot product approximates true dot": verifies mean |error| < 0.15

This is an upstream bug in botirk38/turboquant — the same broken
decode/dot exists in upstream/master with a tolerance of 50.0 that
masked the issue.

BREAKING: encoded data from v0.1.x is still valid, but decode() and
dot() now return correct values instead of near-random ones.

+[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/turboquant-wasm)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/turboquant-wasm)

+

 Based on the paper "TurboQuant: Online Vector Quantization with Near-optimal Distortion Rate".
